solution.p: To clear fractions in an equation, multiply each side of the equation by a common denominator. For this problem, a common denominator is {\left(x+6\right)\mathopen{}\left(x-6\right)}.
We must check solutions when we solve rational equations. In the original equation, both denominators cannot be 0. We set them to 0, so we can find out what values to exclude from the solution set.
We must exclude -6 and 6 from the solution set. These values are not in the solution set we got, so the final solution is x=-4.
